+{"id":"bd-e7ou","title":"Fix --as flag: uses title instead of ID in mol bond","description":"In bondProtoProto, the --as flag is documented as 'Custom ID for compound proto' but the implementation uses it as the title, not the issue ID.

**Current behavior (mol.go:637-638):**
```go
if customID != '' {
    compoundTitle = customID  // Used as title, not ID
}
```

**Options:**
1. Change flag description to say 'Custom title' (documentation fix)
2. Actually use it as a custom ID prefix or full ID (feature change)
3. Add separate --title flag and make --as actually set ID

Recommend option 1 for simplest fix - change 'Custom ID' to 'Custom title' in the flag description.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:22:59.069368-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:22:59.069368-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-elqd","title":"Systematic bd sync stability investigation","description":"## Context

bd sync has chronic instability issues that have persisted since inception:
- issues.jsonl is always dirty after push
- bd sync often creates messes requiring manual cleanup
- Problems escalating despite accumulated bug fixes
- Workarounds are getting increasingly draconian

## Goal

Systematically observe and diagnose bd sync failures rather than applying band-aid fixes.

## Approach

1. Start fresh session with latest binary (all fixes applied)
2. Run bd sync and carefully observe what happens
3. Document exact sequence of events when things go wrong
4. File specific issues for each discrete problem identified
5. Track the root causes, not just symptoms

## Test Environment

- Fresh clone or clean state
- Latest bd binary with all bug fixes
- Monitor both local and remote JSONL state
- Check for timing issues, race conditions, merge conflicts

## Success Criteria

- Identify root causes of sync instability
- Create actionable issues for each problem
- Eventually achieve stable bd sync (no manual intervention needed)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T22:57:25.35289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-eyrh","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: Review remaining beads PRs","description":"## Current State
Just merged PR #653 (doctor refactor) and added tests to restore coverage.

## Remaining Open PRs to Review
Run `gh pr list --repo steveyegge/beads` to see current list. As of handoff:

1. #655 - feat: Linear Integration (jblwilliams)
2. #651 - feat(audit): agent audit trail (dchichkov)
3. #648 - Stop init creating redundant @AGENTS.md (maphew)
4. #646 - fix(unix): handle Statfs field types (jordanhubbard)
5. #645 - feat: /plan-to-beads Claude Code command (petebytes)
6. #642, #641, #640 - sync branch fixes (cpdata)

## Review Checklist
- Check CI status with `gh pr checks <num> --repo steveyegge/beads`
- Verify no .beads/ data leaking (we have a hook now)
- Review code quality
- Merge good ones, request changes on problematic ones

## Notes
- User wants us to be proactive about merging good PRs
- Can add tests ourselves if coverage drops","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-19T17:44:34.149837-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:28:32.428731-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T17:53:20.386937-08:00","sender":"Steve Yegge","ephemeral":true}
 {"id":"bd-eyto","title":"Time-dependent tests may be flaky near TTL boundary","description":"Several tombstone merge tests use time.Now() to create test data: time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour), time.Now().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour), etc. While these work reliably in practice (24h vs 30d TTL has large margin), they could theoretically be flaky if: 1) Tests run slowly, 2) System clock changes during test, 3) TTL constants change. Recommendation: Consider using a fixed reference time or time injection for deterministic tests. Lower priority since current margin is large. Files: internal/merge/merge_test.go:1337-1338, 1352-1353, 1548-1549, 1590-1591","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-hy9p","title":"Add --body-file flag to bd create for reading descriptions from files","description":"## Problem

Creating issues with long/complex descriptions via CLI requires shell escaping gymnastics:

```bash
# Current workaround - awkward heredoc quoting
bd create --title="..." --description="$(cat <<'EOF'
...markdown...
EOF
)"

# Often fails with quote escaping errors in eval context
# Agents resort to writing temp files then reading them
```

## Proposed Solution

Add `--body-file` and `--description-file` flags to read description from a file, matching `gh` CLI pattern.

```bash
# Natural pattern that aligns with training data
cat > /tmp/desc.md << 'EOF'
...markdown content...
EOF

bd create --title="..." --body-file=/tmp/desc.md
```

## Implementation

### 1. Add new flags to `bd create`

```go
createCmd.Flags().String("body-file", "", "Read description from file (use - for stdin)")
createCmd.Flags().String("description-file", "", "Alias for --body-file")
```

### 2. Flag precedence

- If `--body-file` or `--description-file` is provided, read from file
- If value is `-`, read from stdin
- Otherwise fall back to `--body` or `--description` flag
- If neither provided, description is empty (current behavior)

### 3. Error handling

- File doesn't exist → clear error message
- File not readable → clear error message
- stdin specified but not available → clear error message

## Benefits

✅ **Matches training data**: `gh issue create --body-file file.txt` is a common pattern
✅ **No shell escaping issues**: File content is read directly
✅ **Works with any content**: Markdown, special characters, quotes, etc.
✅ **Agent-friendly**: Agents already write complex content to temp files
✅ **User-friendly**: Easier for humans too when pasting long descriptions

## Related Commands

Consider adding similar support to:
- `bd update --body-file` (for updating descriptions)
- `bd comment --body-file` (if/when we add comments)

## Examples

```bash
# From file
bd create --title="Add new feature" --body-file=feature.md

# From stdin
echo "Quick description" | bd create --title="Bug fix" --body-file=-

# With other flags
bd create \
  --title="Security issue" \
  --type=bug \
  --priority=0 \
  --body-file=security-report.md \
  --label=security
```

## Testing

- Test with normal files
- Test with stdin (`-`)
- Test with non-existent files (error handling)
- Test with binary files (should handle gracefully)
- Test with empty files (valid - empty description)
- Test that `--description-file` and `--body-file` are equivalent aliases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-22T00:02:08.762684-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.536024-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:28:52.505239-08:00"}

+{"id":"bd-ia3g","title":"BondRef.ProtoID field name is misleading for mol+mol bonds","description":"In bondMolMol, the BondRef.ProtoID field is used to store molecule IDs:

```go
BondedFrom: append(molA.BondedFrom, types.BondRef{
    ProtoID:   molB.ID,  // This is a molecule, not a proto
    ...
})
```

This is semantically confusing since ProtoID suggests it should only hold proto references.

**Options:**
1. Rename ProtoID to SourceID (breaking change, needs migration)
2. Add documentation clarifying ProtoID can hold molecule IDs in bond context
3. Leave as-is, accept the naming is imprecise

Low priority since it's just naming, not functionality.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:23:00.755067-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:23:00.755067-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-icfe","title":"gt spawn/crew setup should create .beads/redirect for worktrees","description":"Crew clones and polecats need a .beads/redirect file pointing to the shared beads database (../../mayor/rig/.beads). Currently:

- redirect files can get deleted by git clean
- not auto-created during gt spawn or worktree setup
- missing redirects cause 'no beads database found' errors

Found missing in: gastown/joe, beads/zoey (after git clean)

Fix options:
1. gt spawn creates redirect during worktree setup
2. gt prime regenerates missing redirects
3. bd commands auto-detect worktree and find shared beads

This should be standard Gas Town rig configuration.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:30:26.115872-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:30:26.115872-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-kwro.8","title":"Hooks System","description":"Implement hook system for extensibility.

Hook directory: .beads/hooks/
Hook files (executable scripts):
- on_create - runs after bd create
- on_update - runs after bd update  
- on_close - runs after bd close
- on_message - runs after bd mail send

Hook invocation:
- Pass issue ID as first argument
- Pass event type as second argument
- Pass JSON issue data on stdin
- Run asynchronously (dont block command)

Example hook (GGT notification):
  #!/bin/bash
  gt notify --event=$2 --issue=$1

This allows GGT to register notification handlers without Beads knowing about GGT.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T03:02:23.086393-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

New flag:
- bd cleanup --ephemeral - deletes all CLOSED issues with ephemeral=true

Behavior:
- Only deletes if status=closed AND ephemeral=true
- Respects --dry-run flag
- Reports count of deleted ephemeral issues

+{"id":"bd-kzda","title":"Implement conditional bond type for mol bond","description":"The mol bond command accepts 'conditional' as a bond type but doesn't implement any conditional-specific behavior. It currently behaves identically to 'parallel'.

**Expected behavior:**
Conditional bonds should mean 'B runs only if A fails' per the help text (mol.go:318).

**Implementation needed:**
- Add failure-condition dependency handling
- Possibly new dependency type or status-based blocking
- Update bondProtoProto, bondProtoMol, bondMolMol to handle conditional

**Alternative:**
Remove 'conditional' from valid bond types until implemented.

This is new functionality, not a regression.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:23:01.966367-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:23:01.966367-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-lfak","title":"bd preflight: PR readiness checks for contributors","description":"## Vision

Encode project-specific institutional knowledge into executable checks. CONTRIBUTING.md is documentation that's read once and forgotten; `bd preflight` is documentation that runs at exactly the right moment.

## Problem Statement

Contributors face a "last mile" problem - they do the work but stumble on project-specific gotchas at PR time:
- Nix vendorHash gets stale when go.sum changes
- Beads artifacts leak into PRs (see bd-umbf for namespace solution)
- Version mismatches between version.go and default.nix
- Tests/lint not run locally before pushing
- Other project-specific checks that only surface when CI fails

These are too obscure to remember, exist in docs nobody reads end-to-end, and waste CI round-trips.

## Why beads?

Beads already has a foothold in the contributor workflow. It knows:
- Git state (staged files, branch, dirty status)
- Project structure
- The specific issue being worked on
- Project-specific configuration

## Proposed Interface

### Tier 1: Checklist Mode (v1)

    $ bd preflight
    PR Readiness Checklist:

    [ ] Tests pass: go test -short ./...
    [ ] Lint passes: golangci-lint run ./...
    [ ] No beads pollution: check .beads/issues.jsonl diff
    [ ] Nix hash current: go.sum unchanged or vendorHash updated
    [ ] Version sync: version.go matches default.nix

    Run 'bd preflight --check' to validate automatically.

### Tier 2: Check Mode (v2)

    $ bd preflight --check
    ✓ Tests pass
    ✓ Lint passes
    ⚠ Beads pollution: 3 issues in diff - are these project issues or personal?
    ✗ Nix hash stale: go.sum changed, vendorHash needs update
      Fix: sha256-KRR6dXzsSw8OmEHGBEVDBOoIgfoZ2p0541T9ayjGHlI=
    ✓ Version sync

    1 error, 1 warning. Run 'bd preflight --fix' to auto-fix where possible.

### Tier 3: Fix Mode (v3)

    $ bd preflight --fix
    ✓ Updated vendorHash in default.nix
    ⚠ Cannot auto-fix beads pollution - manual review needed

## Checks to Implement

| Check | Description | Auto-fixable |
|-------|-------------|--------------|
| tests | Run go test -short ./... | No |
| lint | Run golangci-lint | Partial (gofmt) |
| beads-pollution | Detect personal issues in diff | No (see bd-umbf) |
| nix-hash | Detect stale vendorHash | Yes (if nix available) |
| version-sync | version.go matches default.nix | Yes |
| no-debug | No TODO/FIXME/console.log | Warn only |
| clean-stage | No unintended files staged | Warn only |

## Future: Configuration

Make checks configurable per-project via .beads/preflight.yaml:

    preflight:
      checks:
        - name: tests
          run: go test -short ./...
          required: true
        - name: no-secrets
          pattern: "**/*.env"
          staged: deny
        - name: custom-check
          run: ./scripts/validate.sh

This lets any project using beads define their own preflight checks.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Static Checklist
- Implement bd preflight with hardcoded checklist for beads
- No execution, just prints what to check
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md to reference it

### Phase 2: Automated Checks
- Implement bd preflight --check
- Run tests, lint, detect stale hashes
- Clear pass/fail/warn output

### Phase 3: Auto-fix
- Implement bd preflight --fix
- Fix vendorHash, version sync
- Integrate with bd-umbf solution for pollution

### Phase 4: Configuration
- .beads/preflight.yaml support
- Make it useful for other projects using beads
- Plugin/hook system for custom checks

## Dependencies

- bd-umbf: Namespace isolation for beads pollution (blocking for full solution)

## Success Metrics

- Fewer CI failures on first PR push
- Reduced "fix nix hash" commits
- Contributors report preflight caught issues before CI","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:39.587078-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:39.587078-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfak","depends_on_id":"bd-umbf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:46.059901-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
